# @cleverbrush

> A TypeScript monorepo containing a high-performance schema validation library and supporting packages. `@cleverbrush/schema` is **14/15 benchmarks faster than Zod**, **14 KB gzipped** (3× smaller than Zod v4), and [Standard Schema v1](https://standardschema.dev/) compliant — meaning it works with tRPC, TanStack Form, React Hook Form, T3 Env, Hono, and 50+ tools out of the box.

The key architectural differentiator is **PropertyDescriptors**: every schema exposes a full runtime introspection tree, enabling schema-driven object mapping (`@cleverbrush/mapper`) and headless form generation (`@cleverbrush/react-form`) from a single schema definition. No competitor provides this level of runtime metadata.

Important notes:

- All builders are **immutable** — every method returns a new instance; the original is never mutated
- The **extension system** (`defineExtension` / `withExtensions`) is type-safe and introspectable — not just opaque `.refine()` callbacks
- `InferType<typeof schema>` derives the TypeScript type at compile time
- Standard Schema v1 `['~standard']` getter is present on every builder
- Sub-path imports (`@cleverbrush/schema/string`, `/object`, etc.) provide tree-shakeable bundles (~4 KB gzip per builder)
- `@cleverbrush/mapper`, `@cleverbrush/react-form`, and `@cleverbrush/schema-json` all have `@cleverbrush/schema` as a peer dependency

## Packages

- [API Reference (TypeDoc)](/api-docs/latest/): Full generated API docs for all packages
- [Playground](/playground): Interactive editor with 40+ runnable examples, live type inference, validation output
- [Schema docs](/schema): @cleverbrush/schema — validation library
- [Mapper docs](/mapper): @cleverbrush/mapper — schema-driven object mapping
- [React Form docs](/react-form): @cleverbrush/react-form — headless schema-driven forms
- [Schema JSON docs](/schema-json): @cleverbrush/schema-json — bidirectional JSON Schema interop
- [Migrating from Zod](/migrating-from-zod): Side-by-side migration guide

## @cleverbrush/schema — Core API

Install: `npm install @cleverbrush/schema`

### Builder factory functions

```typescript
import {
  string, number, boolean, date, func, any, nul,
  object, array, tuple, record, union, enumOf, lazy
} from '@cleverbrush/schema';
```

| Factory | Returns | Description |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| `string()` | `StringSchemaBuilder` | String with constraints |
| `number()` | `NumberSchemaBuilder` | Number with constraints |
| `boolean()` | `BooleanSchemaBuilder` | Boolean value |
| `date()` | `DateSchemaBuilder` | `Date` instance |
| `func()` | `FunctionSchemaBuilder` | Function value |
| `any()` | `AnySchemaBuilder` | Any value (like TS `any`) |
| `nul()` | `NullSchemaBuilder` | Exactly `null` |
| `object(props)` | `ObjectSchemaBuilder` | Object with typed properties |
| `array()` | `ArraySchemaBuilder` | Array with optional element schema |
| `tuple([...schemas])` | `TupleSchemaBuilder` | Fixed-length array, per-position types |
| `record(keySchema, valueSchema)` | `RecordSchemaBuilder` | Dynamic-key `Record<K, V>` |
| `union(schema)` | `UnionSchemaBuilder` | Union type, chain via `.or()` |
| `enumOf(...values)` | `StringSchemaBuilder` | String enum (sugar for `string().oneOf(...)`) |
| `lazy(() => schema)` | `LazySchemaBuilder` | Recursive/self-referential schemas |
| `generic()` | `GenericSchemaBuilder` | Parameterized schemas (constraints applied later) |
| `extern(zodSchema)` | `ExternSchemaBuilder` | Wraps any Standard Schema-compatible validator (Zod, Valibot, etc.) |

### Base SchemaBuilder methods (all builders inherit)

```typescript
const schema = string()
  .optional()                        // T | undefined
  .required('Field is required')     // back to required (with optional error msg)
  .nullable()                        // T | null
  .default('fallback')               // return default when input is undefined
  .default(() => new Date())         // factory function default
  .catch('fallback')                 // return fallback when validation fails (never throws)
  .describe('Human-readable label')  // metadata, flows into IDE tooltips + JSON Schema
  .brand<'UserId'>()                 // branded type
  .readonly()                        // Readonly<T> at type level
  .addValidator((val, ctx) => {      // custom sync validator; return string = error
    if (val.includes('bad')) return 'Invalid value';
  })
  .addPreprocessor((val) => val.trim()) // transform before validation
  .clearValidators()                 // remove all custom validators
  .clearPreprocessors()              // remove all preprocessors
  .withExtension('myKey', myValue)   // attach arbitrary metadata
  .getExtension('myKey');            // retrieve metadata
```

**Validation methods:**
```typescript
schema.validate(data)           // → ValidationResult<T> (sync)
schema.validateAsync(data)      // → Promise<ValidationResult<T>>
schema.parse(data)              // → T or throws SchemaValidationError
schema.parseAsync(data)         // → Promise<T> or throws
schema.safeParse(data)          // → { success, data } | { success: false, error }
schema.safeParseAsync(data)     // async variant
schema.introspect()             // → runtime PropertyDescriptor metadata
```

**ValidationResult shape:**
```typescript
type ValidationResult<T> = {
  valid: boolean;
  object: T | undefined;
  errors: ValidationError[];
  getErrorsFor?: (selector: (t) => any) => ValidationError[];
};
```

### string() methods

```typescript
string()
  .minLength(3)
  .maxLength(100)
  .matches(/^[a-z]+$/)
  .email()
  .url()
  .uuid()
  .ip({ version: 'v4' | 'v6' })   // optional version constraint
  .trim()                           // preprocessor: trims whitespace
  .toLowerCase()                    // preprocessor: lowercases
  .nonempty()                       // minLength(1)
  .oneOf('admin', 'user', 'guest') // narrows type to literal union
```

### number() methods

```typescript
number()
  .min(0)
  .max(100)
  .integer()
  .positive()    // > 0
  .negative()    // < 0
  .finite()
  .multipleOf(5)
  .oneOf(1, 2, 3)  // narrows type to literal union
```

### array() methods

```typescript
array()
  .of(string())     // element schema
  .minLength(1)
  .maxLength(10)
  .nonempty()       // minLength(1)
  .unique()         // no duplicate elements
```

### object(props) usage

```typescript
const UserSchema = object({
  id: number().integer(),
  name: string().minLength(1),
  email: string().email().optional(),
  role: enumOf('admin', 'user'),
});

type User = InferType<typeof UserSchema>;
// { id: number; name: string; email?: string; role: 'admin' | 'user' }

// Per-field error access:
const result = UserSchema.validate(data);
result.getErrorsFor((u) => u.email); // ValidationError[] for email field
```

### union() usage

```typescript
const Schema = union(string()).or(number()).or(nul());
// type: string | number | null
```

### tuple() usage

```typescript
const Point = tuple([number(), number()]);
const RGB = tuple([number(), number(), number()]).rest(number());
```

### record() usage

```typescript
const Dict = record(string(), number());
// type: Record<string, number>

const result = Dict.validate(data);
// result.getNestedErrors() for per-key errors
```

### lazy() for recursive schemas

```typescript
type Tree = { value: number; children: Tree[] };
const TreeSchema: SchemaBuilder<Tree> = object({
  value: number(),
  children: array().of(lazy(() => TreeSchema)),
});
```

### Extension system

Extensions add reusable methods to schema builders (like plugins). They are type-safe and introspectable.

```typescript
import { defineExtension, withExtensions, StringSchemaBuilder } from '@cleverbrush/schema';

// 1. Define extension: adds .slug() method to StringSchemaBuilder
const slugExtension = defineExtension('slug', function(this: StringSchemaBuilder) {
  return this.matches(/^[a-z0-9-]+$/).withExtension('slug', true);
});

// 2. Apply to builder class — returns a factory function
const { string: extString } = withExtensions(StringSchemaBuilder, [slugExtension]);

// 3. Use it
const SlugSchema = extString().slug(); // type-safe, introspectable
SlugSchema.introspect().extensions.slug // → true
```

Extension metadata auto-inference:
- 0 args → extension value is `true`
- 1 arg → extension value is that argument
- N args → extension value is array of arguments
- Manual: call `this.withExtension(key, value)` explicitly

### generic() — Parameterized schemas

```typescript
import { generic, object, string, number } from '@cleverbrush/schema';

// Define a generic "paginated" wrapper
const Paginated = generic((T) =>
  object({
    items: array().of(T),
    total: number().integer(),
    page: number().integer(),
  })
);

// Apply it
const UserPage = Paginated(UserSchema);
type UserPage = InferType<typeof UserPage>;
// { items: User[]; total: number; page: number }
```

### extern() — Wrap external validators

```typescript
import { extern } from '@cleverbrush/schema';
import { z } from 'zod';

// Wrap a Zod schema for use inside Cleverbrush schemas
const ZodEmail = extern(z.string().email());

const MySchema = object({
  email: ZodEmail,           // works in Cleverbrush object schemas
  name: string().minLength(1),
});
```

### Discriminated unions (automatic)

```typescript
const Cat = object({
  type: string().equals('cat'),
  meows: boolean()
});

const Dog = object({
  type: string().equals('dog'),
  barks: boolean()
});

const Animal = union(Cat).or(Dog);
// Discriminator on 'type' detected automatically — O(1) validation
// Validates against the matching branch directly
```

### Type inference

```typescript
import { InferType } from '@cleverbrush/schema';

const OrderSchema = object({
  id: string().uuid(),
  items: array().of(object({ sku: string(), qty: number().integer() })),
  total: number().positive(),
});

type Order = InferType<typeof OrderSchema>;
// Fully inferred — no manual type declaration needed
```

### Standard Schema v1 interop

Every builder exposes `['~standard']` getter, enabling plug-in use with:
- **tRPC**: procedure input/output validation
- **TanStack Form**: `standardSchemaValidator(schema)`
- **React Hook Form**: `standardSchemaResolver(schema)`
- **T3 Env**: `createEnv({ server: { PORT: number().integer() } })`
- **Hono**: request body / query validation middleware

```typescript
const schema = string().email();
const standard = schema['~standard'];
// standard.version === 1
// standard.vendor === '@cleverbrush/schema'
// standard.validate(value) → { value } | { issues: [{ message, path }] }
```

### Sub-path imports (tree-shaking)

```typescript
import { string } from '@cleverbrush/schema/string';  // ~3.8 KB gz
import { number } from '@cleverbrush/schema/number';  // ~3.8 KB gz
import { object } from '@cleverbrush/schema/object';  // ~5.8 KB gz
import { array }  from '@cleverbrush/schema/array';   // ~4.0 KB gz
// Full index: @cleverbrush/schema                    // ~14 KB gz
// Core (no extensions): @cleverbrush/schema/core     // ~12.6 KB gz
```

## @cleverbrush/mapper — Schema-Driven Object Mapping

Install: `npm install @cleverbrush/mapper @cleverbrush/schema`

Compile-time complete mapping of objects between two schemas. Unmapped properties are TypeScript type errors, not runtime surprises. Uses PropertyDescriptor selectors like C# expression trees.

```typescript
import { mapper } from '@cleverbrush/mapper';
import { object, string, number } from '@cleverbrush/schema';

const UserSchema = object({ id: number(), name: string(), email: string() });
const DtoSchema  = object({ userId: string(), displayName: string() });

const registry = mapper()
  .configure(UserSchema, DtoSchema, (m) =>
    m
      .for((dto) => dto.userId).compute((user) => String(user.id))
      .for((dto) => dto.displayName).from((user) => user.name)
  );

const mapUser = registry.getMapper(UserSchema, DtoSchema);
const dto = await mapUser(user);  // type-safe async mapping
```

**Key API:**
- `mapper()` — creates empty `MappingRegistry`
- `.configure(fromSchema, toSchema, callback)` — registers a mapping; callback receives `Mapper` whose `.for((t) => t.prop)` returns `PropertyMappingBuilder`
- `PropertyMappingBuilder` methods: `.from((s) => s.prop)`, `.compute((src) => expr)`, `.ignore()`
- `.getMapper(fromSchema, toSchema)` — returns `(source) => Promise<target>`
- Auto-maps same-name properties with compatible `InferType` primitives

## @cleverbrush/react-form — Headless Schema-Driven Forms

Install: `npm install @cleverbrush/react-form @cleverbrush/schema react`

UI-agnostic, headless form system. Register renderers once globally; fields bind to schema properties via type-safe function selectors (no string names).

```typescript
// 1. Register renderers once at app root:
import { FormSystemProvider } from '@cleverbrush/react-form';

<FormSystemProvider renderers={{
  string: ({ value, onChange, error, label }) => (
    <div>
      <label>{label}</label>
      <input value={value ?? ''} onChange={(e) => onChange(e.target.value)} />
      {error && <span>{error}</span>}
    </div>
  ),
  'string:password': ({ value, onChange }) => (
    <input type="password" value={value ?? ''} onChange={(e) => onChange(e.target.value)} />
  ),
  number: ({ value, onChange }) => (
    <input type="number" value={value ?? ''} onChange={(e) => onChange(Number(e.target.value))} />
  ),
}}>
  <App />
</FormSystemProvider>

// 2. Define schema and create form:
import { useSchemaForm, FormProvider, Field } from '@cleverbrush/react-form';

const LoginSchema = object({ email: string().email(), password: string().minLength(8) });

function LoginForm() {
  const form = useSchemaForm(LoginSchema);

  return (
    <FormProvider form={form}>
      <Field forProperty={(f) => f.email} label="Email" />
      <Field forProperty={(f) => f.password} label="Password" variant="password" />
      <button onClick={() => form.submit()}>Login</button>
    </FormProvider>
  );
}
```

**Key API:**
- `useSchemaForm(schema, options?)` → `SchemaFormInstance`
- `SchemaFormInstance`: `.submit()`, `.validate()`, `.reset(values?)`, `.getValue()`, `.setValue(partial)`, `.useField((tree) => tree.field)`
- `useField(selector, form)` → `{ value, onChange, onBlur, error, touched, dirty }`
- `Field` props: `forProperty`, `form`, `variant`, `label`, `name`, `fieldProps`
- `useFormSystem()` — access global renderer config

## @cleverbrush/schema-json — Bidirectional JSON Schema

Install: `npm install @cleverbrush/schema-json @cleverbrush/schema`

```typescript
import { toJsonSchema, fromJsonSchema, InferFromJsonSchema } from '@cleverbrush/schema-json';

// Schema → JSON Schema
const jsonSchema = toJsonSchema(UserSchema);
// { type: 'object', properties: { ... }, required: [...] }

// JSON Schema → Schema (with full TypeScript inference)
const schema = fromJsonSchema({
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    name: { type: 'string', minLength: 1 },
    age:  { type: 'integer', minimum: 0 },
  },
  required: ['name'],
} as const);  // 'as const' required for precise type inference

// Type inference from JSON Schema literal (no runtime call):
type User = InferFromJsonSchema<typeof jsonSchemaLiteral>;
```

**Supported JSON Schema keywords:** `type`, `properties`, `required`, `additionalProperties`, `const`, `enum`, `anyOf`, `minLength`, `maxLength`, `pattern`, `minimum`, `maximum`, `exclusiveMinimum`, `exclusiveMaximum`, `multipleOf`, `minItems`, `maxItems`, `format` (email/uuid/uri/url/ipv4/ipv6/date-time), `readOnly`, `description`.

## @cleverbrush/deep — Deep Utilities

Install: `npm install @cleverbrush/deep`

```typescript
import { deepEqual, deepExtend, deepFlatten, HashObject } from '@cleverbrush/deep';

deepEqual(a, b)                           // recursive equality; handles Date, circular refs
deepEqual(a, b, { disregardArrayOrder: true })
deepExtend(obj1, obj2, obj3)              // deep merge → new object (immutable)
deepFlatten({ a: { b: { c: 1 } } })      // → { 'a.b.c': 1 }
deepFlatten(obj, '_')                     // custom delimiter
HashObject({ name: 'Alice', age: 30 })   // → stable hash string
```

## @cleverbrush/async — Async Utilities

Install: `npm install @cleverbrush/async`

```typescript
import { Collector, debounce, throttle, retry } from '@cleverbrush/async';

// Collect named key/value pairs, resolve when all keys received:
const c = new Collector<{ a: string; b: number }>(['a', 'b'], 5000 /* timeout ms */);
c.collect('a', 'hello').collect('b', 42);
const result = await c.toPromise(); // { a: 'hello', b: 42 }

// Debounce / throttle:
const debouncedSave = debounce(save, 300);
const throttledScroll = throttle(onScroll, 100);

// Retry with exponential backoff:
await retry(fetchData, {
  maxRetries: 3,
  minDelay: 100,
  delayFactor: 2,
  shouldRetry: (err) => err.status >= 500,
});
```

## @cleverbrush/scheduler — Job Scheduler

Install: `npm install @cleverbrush/scheduler`

Cron-like scheduler that runs tasks in **worker threads**. Schema-validated job definitions. Requires Node.js 16+.

```typescript
import { JobScheduler } from '@cleverbrush/scheduler';

const scheduler = new JobScheduler({
  rootFolder: './jobs',  // folder containing job worker files
});

scheduler.addJob({
  id: 'daily-report',
  path: 'daily-report.js',
  schedule: { every: 'day', interval: 1, hour: 9, minute: 0 },
  timeout: 1000 * 60 * 5,
  maxRetries: 3,
  noConcurrentRuns: true,
});

scheduler.start();
```

**Schedule types:** `{ every: 'minute', interval: N }`, `{ every: 'day', interval: N, hour, minute }`, `{ every: 'week', interval: N, dayOfWeek: [0-6], hour, minute }`, `{ every: 'month', interval: N, dayOfMonth, hour, minute }`, `{ every: 'year', interval: N, month, dayOfMonth, hour, minute }`.

**Events:** `job:start`, `job:end`, `job:error`, `job:timeout`, `job:message` — each with `{ jobId, instanceId, ... }`.

**Persistence:** Default in-memory; implement `IJobRepository` for database-backed storage.

## Optional

- [Playground: Standard Schema with TanStack Form](/showcases/tanstack-form): Live showcase — 5-field registration form using `standardSchemaValidator`
- [Playground: Standard Schema with T3 Env](/showcases/t3-env): Live showcase — `createEnv` with server + client env validation
- [Migrating from Zod](/migrating-from-zod): Side-by-side API comparison and migration patterns
- [Benchmark results](https://github.com/cleverbrush/framework): 14/15 benchmarks faster than Zod — 1.2–2.65x faster on valid input, 8–204x faster on invalid input
